pattern: A query matches when the pattern is a subsequence of it AND every uppercase letter in the query is consumed by the pattern, since only lowercase letters may be inserted.
hints: You may only insert lowercase letters, so every uppercase letter that appears in a query must be matched by an uppercase letter in the pattern.
Walk the query left to right with a pointer into the pattern. Advance the pattern pointer whenever the characters match.

If a query character is uppercase but does not equal the current pattern character, it cannot have been inserted, so the match fails. At the end, the pattern pointer must have reached the pattern's end.

edge_inputs.description: uppercase-only pattern as a subsequence check
pattern with lowercase forces those exact lowercase letters
extra uppercase in query with none in pattern fails
pattern longer than query is impossible

stress.description: random mixed-case queries against a random pattern
